title: Complaining at the Pub
learningGoal: The learner will listen to a conversational dialogue to comprehend the causative-passive form in its most natural habitat: complaining about work.
instructions: Listen to two colleagues talking at an izakaya (Japanese pub) after work. Identify what the speaker was forced to do.

contentItems.text: Listen for 'saseraremashita' (forced to do) and 'nomasaremashita' (forced to drink).

listeningGoal: Identify the actions the speaker was forced to do against their will by their boss.
audioScenario: Two colleagues chatting after a long day.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: はい。きのう、ぶちょうに ざんぎょうを させられました。
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Hai. Kinou, buchou ni zangyou wo saseraremashita.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Yes. Yesterday, I was forced to do overtime by the boss.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: ええ。そして、さけを のまされました。
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Yeah. And, I was forced to drink alcohol.

audioTranscriptFull:
（同僚A）つかれましたね。
（同僚B）はい。きのう、ぶちょうに ざんぎょうを させられました。
（同僚A）たいへんですね。
（同僚B）ええ。そして、さけを のまされました。

questionSet.questionText: How does the second speaker look/feel?
What was the speaker forced to do yesterday?
Who forced them to do it?
What else was the speaker forced to do?
Write the romaji for the phrase 'forced to do' heard in the second sentence.

feedbackRules.onPass: Perfect listening! You successfully identified the causative-passive complaints.
feedbackRules.onFail: Listen again carefully. 'Saseraremashita' = forced to do. 'Nomasaremashita' = forced to drink.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Notice how 'Nomasaremashita' is used instead of the longer 'Nomaseraremashita'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: つかれましたね。はい。きのう、ぶちょうに ざんぎょうを させられました。たいへんですね。ええ。そして、さけを のまされました。

commonMistakes: Thinking the speaker made the boss do overtime (confusing the causative with the causative-passive).
